query: The main reason is a change of plans. I was actually out of town right after booking, so I wasn’t able to look into this until now. Could you please check if there’s any way to cancel and get a refund? I really don’t want to cancel if I won’t get any money back.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user asks to cancel a reservation.
assistant: The assistant asks for the user ID and reservation ID.
user: The user provides user ID emma_kim_9957 and reservation ID EHGLP3.
assistant called tool: get_reservation_details for reservation EHGLP3.
assistant: The assistant summarizes that reservation EHGLP3 is a basic-economy one-way trip with no insurance and asks for the cancellation reason while noting refundability depends on policy.

candidate_answers.text: I’ll check the flight status for reservation EHGLP3 so I can confirm whether a refundable cancellation is even possible before you decide.
The fare type and lack of insurance are visible, but cancellation eligibility still depends on whether any flight segments have already departed, so checking flight status is the grounded next move.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user turn where the reservation details and cancellation reason are visible, but refundability still hinges on whether the booked flights have departed, making flight-status checking the grounded next move.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user’s request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query comes directly from the selected user message, not from task hidden fields
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes future assistant or tool outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
best_action is get_flight_status because the assistant needs flight-status evidence before ruling on cancellation/refund eligibility
